title: [cross-impl] EDHOC exporter KDF label encoding mismatch (int vs tstr)
description: Python (edhoc.py:464 uses int 7/10/0/1 -> cbor2 uint) and Zephyr (lichen/subsys/lichen/edhoc/edhoc.c:edhoc_export_kdf uses zcbor_uint32_put) encode exporter labels as CBOR integers. Rust (lichen-oscore/src/edhoc.rs:321-333 passes "7","10","0","1" -> edhoc_kdf encodes as tstr major type 3). Causes divergent master_secret/salt derivation despite shared test vectors and RFC 9528 §7.2.1. See edhoc_initiator_export_oscore, export_context, _edhoc_kdf. Breaks interop. Vectors in test/vectors/edhoc.json and lichen-oscore/tests/vectors.rs:545 do not align all three impls. Fix: standardize on integer labels per Python/Zephyr/RFC exporter spec across all impls (update Rust edhoc_kdf label handling for numeric labels).
